mechanism_narrative: High and rapidly rising unemployment signals severe economic contraction, leading to lower corporate earnings expectations and negative S&P 500 forward returns as consumer spending power diminishes and business confidence wanes.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 7.2% and rising rapidly in late 2008/early 2009
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of periods where UNRATE is rising, or the Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate approaches zero, it signals extreme monetary easing to combat economic downturns. This aggressive policy eventually supports asset prices by lowering the cost of capital and encouraging risk-taking, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns.
regime_hint: deep recession with aggressive monetary easing
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces negative 60d returns in > 50% of periods where FEDFUNDS is below 0.25%, or the Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window in such periods.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of declining retail sales and rising unemployment indicates a severe contraction in consumer demand and labor market distress. This significantly dampens corporate revenues and profits, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:declining retail sales and rising unemployment in late 2008/early 2009
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20d returns in > 50% of triggered instances, or the interaction term's coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: In a recessionary environment, a continued decline in industrial production signals persistent weakness in the manufacturing sector, indicating a lack of recovery momentum and leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns due to reduced corporate output and investment.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5d returns in > 50% of triggered instances during recessionary periods, or the signal's predictive power diminishes outside of recessions.

mechanism_narrative: A significant and sustained drop in housing starts, while initially negative, eventually signals a bottoming process in the housing market. This stabilization is a prerequisite for broader economic recovery and subsequent positive S&P 500 forward returns as confidence returns.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST very low at 625.0k in late 2008/early 2009
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces negative 60d returns in > 50% of triggered instances, or housing starts continue to decline significantly after the trigger.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent low or negative inflation (CPIAUCSL) during an economic downturn indicates weak demand and potential deflationary spirals. This can depress corporate earnings and lead to negative S&P 500 forward returns as pricing power erodes.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL showing deflationary pressure (213.06) in late 2008/early 2009
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20d returns in > 50% of triggered instances, or CPIAUCSL turns positive and accelerates while SPX still declines.

mechanism_narrative: A deceleration in the rate of job losses (PAYEMS) suggests a stabilization in the labor market. This is a leading indicator for economic recovery and improved consumer confidence, ultimately supporting positive S&P 500 forward returns as the economy bottoms out.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces negative 60d returns in > 50% of triggered instances, or PAYEMS continues to decline significantly after the trigger.

mechanism_narrative: Extremely low federal funds rate combined with severely depressed retail sales indicates a crisis-level demand shock met with aggressive monetary easing. This combination suggests a potential turning point where policy support eventually overcomes the demand weakness, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS near zero and RSAFS declining sharply in late 2008/early 2009
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces negative 60d returns in > 50% of triggered instances, or the interaction term's coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).
